runs.reasoningSummary: High-income primary earners (decile 9) exhibit moderate labor supply elasticity due to weaker income effects and greater responsiveness to marginal wages than lower deciles; draws from upper-end taxable income elasticity estimates scaled down for intensive margin in simulations.
